# sweep

> Extending `broom` to time series forecasting

The `sweep` package extends the `broom` tools (tidy, glance, and
augment) for performing forecasts and time series analysis in the
“tidyverse”. The package is geared towards “tidying” the forecast
workflow used with Rob Hyndman’s `forecast` package.

## Benefits

- **Designed for modeling and scaling forecasts using the the
  `tidyverse` tools in [*R for Data Science*](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/)**
- **Extends `broom` for model analysis (ARIMA, ETS, BATS, etc)**
- **Tidies the `forecast` objects for easy plotting and “tidy” data
  manipulation**
- **Integrates `timetk` to enable dates and datetimes (irregular time
  series) in the tidied forecast output**

## Tools

The package contains the following elements:

1.  **model tidiers**: `sw_tidy`, `sw_glance`, `sw_augment`,
    `sw_tidy_decomp` functions extend `tidy`, `glance`, and `augment`
    from the `broom` package specifically for models (`ets()`,
    `Arima()`, `bats()`, etc) used for forecasting.

2.  **forecast tidier**: `sw_sweep` converts a `forecast` object to a
    tibble that can be easily manipulated in the “tidyverse”.

## Making forecasts in the tidyverse

`sweep` enables converting a `forecast` object to `tibble`. The result
is ability to use `dplyr`, `tidyr`, and `ggplot` natively to manipulate,
analyze and visualize forecasts.

![](reference/figures/forecast.png)

## Forecasting multiple time series groups at scale

Often forecasts are required on grouped data to analyse trends in
sub-categories. The good news is scaling from one time series to many is
easy with the various `sw_` functions in combination with `dplyr` and
`purrr`.

![](reference/figures/time_series_groups.png)

## Forecasting multiple models for accuracy

A common goal in forecasting is to compare different forecast models
against each other. `sweep` helps in this area as well.

![](reference/figures/multiple_models.png)

## broom extensions for forecasting

If you are familiar with `broom`, you know how useful it is for
retrieving “tidy” format model components. `sweep` extends this benefit
to the `forecast` package workflow with the following functions:

- `sw_tidy`: Returns model coefficients (single column)
- `sw_glance`: Returns accuracy statistics (single row)
- `sw_augment`: Returns residuals
- `sw_tidy_decomp`: Returns seasonal decompositions
- `sw_sweep`: Returns tidy forecast outputs.

The compatibility chart is listed below.

| Object      | sw_tidy() | sw_glance() | sw_augment() | sw_tidy_decomp() | sw_sweep() |
|:------------|:---------:|:-----------:|:------------:|:----------------:|:----------:|
| ar          |           |             |              |                  |            |
| arima       |     X     |      X      |      X       |                  |            |
| Arima       |     X     |      X      |      X       |                  |            |
| ets         |     X     |      X      |      X       |        X         |            |
| baggedETS   |           |             |              |                  |            |
| bats        |     X     |      X      |      X       |        X         |            |
| tbats       |     X     |      X      |      X       |        X         |            |
| nnetar      |     X     |      X      |      X       |                  |            |
| stl         |           |             |              |        X         |            |
| HoltWinters |     X     |      X      |      X       |        X         |            |
| StructTS    |     X     |      X      |      X       |        X         |            |
| tslm        |     X     |      X      |      X       |                  |            |
| decompose   |           |             |              |        X         |            |
| adf.test    |     X     |      X      |              |                  |            |
| Box.test    |     X     |      X      |              |                  |            |
| kpss.test   |     X     |      X      |              |                  |            |
| forecast    |           |             |              |                  |     X      |

Function Compatibility

## Installation

Here’s how to get started.

Development version with latest features:

``` r
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("business-science/sweep")
```

## Further Information

The `sweep` package includes several vignettes to help users get up to
speed quickly:

- SW00 - Introduction to `sweep`
- SW01 - Forecasting Time Series Groups in the tidyverse
- SW02 - Forecasting Using Multiple Models

# Package index

## All functions

- [`add_index()`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/add_index.md)
  : Adds a sequential index column to a data frame
- [`arima_string()`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/arima_string.md)
  : Print the ARIMA model parameters
- [`bats_string()`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/bats_string.md)
  : Print the BATS model parameters
- [`bike_sales`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/bike_sales.md)
  : Fictional sales data for bike shops purchasing Cannondale bikes
- [`sw_augment()`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/sw_augment.md)
  : Augment data according to a tidied model
- [`sw_augment(`*`<default>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/sw_augment.default.md)
  : Default augment method
- [`sw_augment_columns()`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/sw_augment_columns.md)
  : Augments data
- [`sw_glance()`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/sw_glance.md)
  : Construct a single row summary "glance" of a model, fit, or other
  object
- [`sw_glance(`*`<default>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/sw_glance.default.md)
  : Default glance method
- [`sw_sweep()`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/sw_sweep.md)
  : Tidy forecast objects
- [`sw_tidy()`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/sw_tidy.md)
  : Tidy the result of a time-series model into a summary tibble
- [`sw_tidy(`*`<default>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/sw_tidy.default.md)
  : Default tidying method
- [`sw_tidy_decomp()`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/sw_tidy_decomp.md)
  : Coerces decomposed time-series objects to tibble format.
- [`tbats_string()`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tbats_string.md)
  : Print the TBATS model parameters
- [`sw_tidy(`*`<HoltWinters>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_HoltWinters.md)
  [`sw_glance(`*`<HoltWinters>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_HoltWinters.md)
  [`sw_augment(`*`<HoltWinters>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_HoltWinters.md)
  [`sw_tidy_decomp(`*`<HoltWinters>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_HoltWinters.md)
  : Tidying methods for HoltWinters modeling of time series
- [`sw_tidy(`*`<StructTS>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_StructTS.md)
  [`sw_glance(`*`<StructTS>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_StructTS.md)
  [`sw_augment(`*`<StructTS>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_StructTS.md)
  : Tidying methods for StructTS (Error, Trend, Seasonal) / exponential
  smoothing modeling of time series
- [`sw_tidy(`*`<Arima>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_arima.md)
  [`sw_glance(`*`<Arima>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_arima.md)
  [`sw_augment(`*`<Arima>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_arima.md)
  [`sw_tidy(`*`<stlm>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_arima.md)
  : Tidying methods for ARIMA modeling of time series
- [`sw_tidy(`*`<bats>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_bats.md)
  [`sw_glance(`*`<bats>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_bats.md)
  [`sw_augment(`*`<bats>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_bats.md)
  [`sw_tidy_decomp(`*`<bats>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_bats.md)
  : Tidying methods for BATS and TBATS modeling of time series
- [`sw_tidy_decomp(`*`<decomposed.ts>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_decomposed_ts.md)
  : Tidying methods for decomposed time series
- [`sw_tidy(`*`<ets>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_ets.md)
  [`sw_glance(`*`<ets>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_ets.md)
  [`sw_augment(`*`<ets>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_ets.md)
  [`sw_tidy_decomp(`*`<ets>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_ets.md)
  : Tidying methods for ETS (Error, Trend, Seasonal) exponential
  smoothing modeling of time series
- [`sw_tidy(`*`<nnetar>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_nnetar.md)
  [`sw_glance(`*`<nnetar>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_nnetar.md)
  [`sw_augment(`*`<nnetar>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_nnetar.md)
  : Tidying methods for Nural Network Time Series models
- [`sw_tidy(`*`<stl>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_stl.md)
  [`sw_tidy_decomp(`*`<stl>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_stl.md)
  [`sw_tidy_decomp(`*`<stlm>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_stl.md)
  [`sw_glance(`*`<stlm>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_stl.md)
  [`sw_augment(`*`<stlm>`*`)`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/tidiers_stl.md)
  : Tidying methods for STL (Seasonal, Trend, Level) decomposition of
  time series
- [`validate_index()`](https://business-science.github.io/sweep/reference/validate_index.md)
  : Validates data frame has column named the same name as variable
  rename_index
